problem.title: Feast of Delicacy
problem.description: After the great exodus from the Tower of God, the maidens’ party wants to celebrate with a huge feast. Hameln the Hero has n pieces of spice placed on a conveyor belt in order, each with deliciousness a_i (which may be negative). She can perform two actions: 1) choose indices l and r (1 ≤ l ≤ r ≤ n) and reverse the segment a_l … a_r; 2) choose a number k (0 ≤ k ≤ n) and use only the first k spices, discarding the rest. The total deliciousness is the sum of the first k spices after the possible reversal (0 if k = 0). Determine the maximum possible deliciousness she can achieve by choosing l, r, and k optimally.
problem.input_format: The first line contains an integer t (1 ≤ t ≤ 10^4), the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer n (2 ≤ n ≤ 2·10^5) – the number of spices. The second line contains n integers a_1, a_2, ..., a_n (−10^9 ≤ a_i ≤ 10^9). It is guaranteed that the sum of n over all test cases does not exceed 2·10^5.
problem.output_format: For each test case, output a single integer – the maximum deliciousness Hameln can obtain.
constraints: Sum of n over all test cases ≤ 2·10^5
problem.tle_strategy: n=max with adversarial order, duplicates, and overflow-prone values

all_testcases.description: Official sample input covering typical mixed values and negative numbers
Minimum size n=2 with extreme opposite values to test overflow handling
Minimal size n=2 with mixed signs (edge case for smallest n)
All elements equal positive value, testing handling of uniform arrays
Strictly decreasing negative numbers, testing reverse sorted negative array
Mixed values with zeros, extremes, and alternating signs, typical random-like case
